task_completion.description: Agent initiated a crawl of the target website using Screaming Frog SEO Spider.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog SEO Spider application window is open with 'https://crawler-test.com/' entered in the URL bar at the top, and the crawl progress bar shows activity or 100% completion.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to view the redirect data (3xx status codes) within Screaming Frog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog UI shows the 'Response Codes' tab filtered by 'Redirection (3xx)', or the 'Redirect Chains' report is visible, displaying URLs with 301/302 status codes.

task_completion.description: Agent exported the redirect response data as a CSV file to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows a CSV file being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory. The file should clearly be an export from Screaming Frog.

task_completion.description: Agent created the redirect report text file in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'redirect_report.txt' located in the '~/Documents/SEO/reports/' directory.

task_completion.description: The report documents the total count of redirecting URLs and the specific status codes present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in 'redirect_report.txt' explicitly mentions a total count of redirecting URLs (which should be > 3) and lists status codes found, specifically including 301 and 302.

task_completion.description: The report documents the number of redirect chains (3 or more hops) and includes remediation recommendations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in 'redirect_report.txt' explicitly mentions the count of redirect chains with 3 or more hops and includes written advice/recommendations on how to consolidate or fix them.

integrity.description: Agent actually used the Screaming Frog GUI to perform the crawl rather than relying on terminal scripts or curl commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog graphical interface being interacted with, not just terminal outputs.

integrity.description: The CSV export was generated by the software, not manually typed or faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV is visible, it should have standard Screaming Frog column headers (e.g., Address, Status Code, Redirect URI) rather than a manually constructed minimal list.

integrity.description: The report contains actual data from the crawl rather than placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should contain specific numbers for total redirects and chains that align with a real crawl of crawler-test.com, not generic text like 'Total Redirects: [Insert Number Here]'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL is https://crawler-test.com/. A genuine Screaming Frog crawl of this site will find well over 3 redirects (at least 28 documented test paths exist), specifically including 301 and 302 status codes. The final report must be located at ~/Documents/SEO/reports/redirect_report.txt and the CSV export at ~/Documents/SEO/exports/.
